c74object.name: lilypond
c74object.description: Use the lilypond object to engrave music notation with GNU LilyPond and draw it in the patcher. Supply the notation as LilyPond source text with the set or read message, and the object renders it to vector graphics and resizes the box to fit. LilyPond is not bundled: an existing installation is located automatically, or pointed to with the lilypondpath attribute.
c74object.discussion: The object does not bundle LilyPond; it drives a separately installed lilypond binary as a subprocess. Install LilyPond yourself (for example with "brew install lilypond" or from lilypond.org), then either let autofind locate it or set lilypondpath explicitly. Use getpath to confirm which binary is active. Notation is rendered on a background thread and redrawn automatically whenever the source or a display attribute changes, so rapid edits stay responsive.

method.name: set
method.description: Joins the message arguments into the LilyPond source and re-renders. Suitable for short snippets without backslash commands, for example set { c' d' e' f' g' }. A Max message box cannot preserve the backslash, brace, or quote characters used by most LilyPond input, so use read for non-trivial scores.

method.name: read
method.digest: Load LilyPond source from a file
method.description: Reads the named .ly file, located on the Max search path, and re-renders. This is the preferred way to supply scores that use backslash commands, which a message box cannot carry.

method.name: getpath
method.digest: Report the active LilyPond binary path
method.description: Posts the path of the lilypond binary that would be used for the next render to the Max console. Useful for confirming which installation is active when lilypondpath is empty and autofind is resolving the binary automatically.

attribute.name: lilypondpath
attribute.description: Absolute path to the lilypond executable used for rendering. When empty and autofind is enabled, common install locations are probed automatically: Homebrew (/opt/homebrew/bin/lilypond, /usr/local/bin/lilypond), the official relocatable app (/Applications/LilyPond.app), MacPorts, and finally $PATH. Set this to use a specific binary regardless of what auto-find would discover. The discovered path is written back to this attribute after the first successful render, so it is visible in the Inspector.

attribute.name: autofind
attribute.description: When enabled (the default), probes common install locations for a lilypond binary whenever lilypondpath is empty. Disable to suppress discovery and require an explicit path.

attribute.name: size
attribute.description: Controls how large the notation appears, expressed as the number of screen pixels per staff-space (the gap between two adjacent staff lines). The default is 8.

attribute.name: margin
attribute.digest: Margin around the notation in pixels
attribute.description: Padding in pixels between the rendered notation and the edges of the box. The box is automatically resized to fit the image plus this margin on all sides. The default is 8.
